subject: Flag CSD's zero promo signal in findings output
description: In 02_thesis_data/_02_preprocessing/nielsen/CSD/pipeline_step_scripts/pre_processing_notebook_csd.ipynb, Step 3.11 (Cross-Brand Heterogeneity) and Step 3.16 (Promo Intensity Distribution) both report 'No promo activity recorded for this category at the region grain' / 'Mean/Median promo intensity: 0.000' for CSD -- an honest finding, not a bug, but it means promo_intensity/promo_units-derived engineered features in Step 4.3 are constant-zero for this category. Add a 'PROMO_SIGNAL_PRESENT' boolean (or similar) to the findings dict built in Step 3.19 (Summary & Save Findings cell), computed from whether df['promo_units'].sum() > 0, with a short rationale string explaining why it's False for CSD. This does not require changing Step 4.3's feature engineering itself (promo_intensity should still be computed and simply be constant 0 for this category) -- it only documents the finding so a downstream modeling notebook or the next category (Danskvand/Energidrikke/RTD, which may have real promo data) isn't surprised by it. Verify by re-running the notebook and checking csd_eda_findings.json contains the new PROMO_SIGNAL_PRESENT key with value False (and its rationale) for CSD.
activeForm: Flagging CSD's zero promo signal in findings output
